summary:
This patient may have a primary cutaneous malignancy due to persistent lesions despite steroid treatment; given the size (>1cm) and appearance suggestive of invasion, it is critical to rule out squamous cell carcinoma—a common type of keratinocyte-driven skin cancer—and obtain biopsy confirmation before initiating any further therapeutic interventions such as immunotherapy or targeted therapies.

Second sentence must emphasize diagnostic priority: ruling out squamous cell carcinoma via biopsy because it arises from epidermal keratinocytes which fits both presentation and classification criteria under current understanding of dermal pathology.

I will avoid mentioning unrelated details such as occupation status ("accountant"), general health background (“unremarkable”), or treatments beyond steroids since those aren't central here.
